nodes.label: Modular Monolith + BFF Pattern
nodes.rationale: Single deployable Express app chosen over microservices: simpler to run/test locally, no inter-service networking overhead, extractable later if scale demands it.

nodes.label: No Supabase SDK on Frontend
nodes.rationale: Every auth flow (email/password, OAuth, logout) goes through Aurevo.BE's /api/auth/* endpoints so the browser never holds a Supabase session directly, making server-side logout reliable and keeping the client bundle free of a second auth system.

nodes.label: Backend-driven Auth via supabaseAdmin.auth.getClaims
nodes.rationale: Supabase signs JWTs with an asymmetric ES256 key by default, so a static-secret jsonwebtoken.verify() would reject valid tokens; getClaims uses cached JWKS instead.

nodes.label: Atomic Guarded Stock Decrement
nodes.rationale: One atomic guarded UPDATE (quantity - N WHERE quantity - reserved_quantity >= N) at checkout instead of reserve-then-decrement, so concurrent checkouts can't oversell; restoreOrderStock shared helper handles cancel/status-change restores.

nodes.label: Order Invoice PDF (pdfkit)
nodes.rationale: Generated with pdfkit rather than a headless-browser render to keep the Railway image light; never persisted so it stays in sync with order state; bundles Noto Sans Bengali static TTF (variable font corrupts fontkit subsetting) and draws the wordmark as vectors via svg-to-pdfkit.

nodes.label: Two-Environment CI/CD Model
nodes.rationale: Local Docker Supabase for dev/test plus one production Supabase, no separate staging project; CI's fresh Docker Postgres on every run acts as ephemeral staging. Gap: no free-tier backups on prod.

nodes.label: Backend-driven OAuth PKCE Implementation
nodes.rationale: PKCE verifier stored keyed by a state value carried inside redirect_to, not the top-level state param, because GoTrue owns that for its own handshake; sending a top-level state breaks every callback with an invalid-state error.

nodes.label: Stock Accounting Double-count Bug Fix
nodes.rationale: An earlier version incremented reserved_quantity and decremented quantity for the same sale, double-counting every order until availability silently hit zero; fixed by a single atomic guarded UPDATE, reserved_quantity now stays 0 by design.

nodes.label: Saved Addresses created_at Ordering Fix
nodes.rationale: Without an explicit ORDER BY, Postgres could return rows in different physical order after an UPDATE (e.g. toggling is_default), visibly swapping card positions in the FE grid; fixed by ordering by created_at ASC.

nodes.label: Cart Limiter Split from authLimiter
nodes.rationale: Sharing authLimiter meant a shopper adding a few items back-to-back could trip a login-abuse limiter; POST /cart/items given its own cartLimiter (60/min).

nodes.label: Gmail SMTP to Resend Migration
nodes.rationale: Earlier version used Gmail SMTP before the team owned a real domain; switched to Resend once aurevofashion.store was purchased and verified, since Gmail's 500/day cap and generic sender were only a stopgap.

nodes.label: hybrid+rerank Retrieval Default
nodes.rationale: pgvector cosine + Postgres FTS fused via RRF, then reranked by Voyage rerank-2.5-lite; eval-gated default because it measured strictly >= vector at MRR 1.000; rerank falls back to fusion order on failure.

nodes.label: Auto-embed Fire-and-forget, Not CDC
nodes.rationale: products.service.ts fires a single-product re-embed after DB writes, keeping kb_chunks fresh without event infra; only scales to the catalog's current small size, full CDC parked in Backlog.

nodes.label: Sliding Window + Rolling Intent Summary
nodes.rationale: Last ~6 turns plus a rolling intent_summary (refreshed every 3 user turns via a cheap Haiku call) sent instead of the full transcript so token cost stays roughly flat on long conversations.

nodes.label: Product Card Matching Algorithm
nodes.rationale: Cards matched against the assistant's own reply text (not every retrieval result) via whitespace-normalized exact match first, then a suffix-stripped fuzzy fallback, with a guard preventing the fuzzy tier re-adding a near-duplicate; needed because catalog titles contain messy suffixes like "1.1" or "{shoe1:1}".

nodes.label: Test Suite Truncates user_addresses Gotcha
nodes.rationale: pnpm test truncates most tables between files including user_addresses; global-teardown.ts self-heals by reseeding the catalog and re-granting the test-admin fixture's profiles row after every run.

nodes.label: CI migrate Job paths-filter Incident
nodes.rationale: dorny/paths-filter must pin base/ref to github.event.before/github.sha explicitly; otherwise a merge-commit push falls back to comparing live dev/main tips, races merge-back.yml, and can silently report 0 changed files even when supabase/migrations/** changed -- kept migration 039 out of production for two merges with CI reporting success.

nodes.label: Local-Only DB Rule
nodes.rationale: Never touch production DB; all changes go to local Supabase Docker only, prod connection string kept as read-only reference.

nodes.label: Order Invoice PDF + Confirmation Email (skills ref)
nodes.norm_label: order invoice pdf + confirmation email (skills ref)

nodes.label: i18n Implemented (not deferred)
nodes.rationale: English/Bangla via i18next, English always default, Bangla opt-in via header toggle only; timezone-based auto-switching to Bangla was deliberately removed for predictability.

nodes.label: No Dedicated Staging Supabase Decision
nodes.rationale: Two-environment model is what the team can afford to operate; CI's fresh disposable Postgres + full test suite covers most schema/logic bugs a staging env would catch. Gap: no free-tier backups on prod, no undo for real data loss.

nodes.label: Resend for Order Confirmation Email Decision
nodes.rationale: Gmail SMTP was a zero-cost stopgap before the team owned a domain; switched to Resend after aurevofashion.store was DNS-verified (SPF/DKIM via Vercel) to drop Gmail's 500/day cap and get real deliverability.
nodes.norm_label: resend for order confirmation email decision

nodes.label: On-demand pdfkit Invoice Generation Decision
nodes.rationale: Never persisted, regenerated on every email/download so payment status and line items stay current; bundled Bengali TTF and vector-drawn wordmark; fixed-width order numbers keep layout stable under concurrency.

nodes.label: Steadfast Courier Integration Decision
nodes.rationale: Booking is an explicit admin action only because it commits real COD/delivery money and Steadfast has no cancel-consignment endpoint; webhook + reconciliation poll cover status; public tracking excludes recipient PII.

nodes.label: AI Chat Rebuilt as Full RAG Pipeline Decision
nodes.rationale: Previously a bare tool-use bot with no retrieval/persistence/real streaming; rebuilt with Voyage embeddings + pgvector, real stream:true, auth-gated order lookup enforced in code (not just prompted), 90d/48h retention via internal cleanup route + Railway cron.
nodes.norm_label: ai chat rebuilt as full rag pipeline decision

nodes.label: Full Auth + Checkout E2E Testing Decision
nodes.norm_label: full auth + checkout e2e testing decision

nodes.label: Modular Monolith + BFF Architecture Pattern
nodes.rationale: Chosen over microservices for simpler local dev/test, no inter-service networking overhead, and easy future extraction if scale demands it.

nodes.label: Express + TypeScript Choice
nodes.rationale: Chosen over NestJS/Fastify/Hono for explicitness (no magic), easy unit testing, and reviewer-readability -- verbosity treated as a feature for a portfolio project.

nodes.label: Drizzle ORM (introspect-first) Choice
nodes.rationale: Chosen over Prisma/TypeORM/raw SQL; schema generated via drizzle-kit introspect from the live DB (source of truth), avoiding schema drift and ORM startup cost.

nodes.label: Supabase (PostgreSQL 15) Choice
nodes.rationale: Built-in Auth (JWT/OAuth/magic links), S3-compatible Storage, and Row Level Security avoid building auth/storage from scratch; local Docker stack gives dev/prod parity.

nodes.label: Zod Validation Choice
nodes.rationale: Chosen over Joi/class-validator/yup for TypeScript-native inferred types (z.infer) as one source of truth for request shapes.

nodes.label: Vitest + Supertest, Real DB over Mocks
nodes.rationale: Mocks pass when prod fails -- e.g. the cart FK chain (cart_items -> profiles -> auth.users) would never be caught by mocked tests; fileParallelism:false avoids FK violations on the shared local DB.
nodes.norm_label: vitest + supertest, real db over mocks

nodes.label: Module Structure (4-file pattern rationale)
nodes.rationale: Services are pure functions testable without HTTP; controllers stay logic-free so the transport layer is swappable; tests import the router directly without spinning up the full server.

nodes.label: Auth Design (JWKS getClaims)
nodes.rationale: Supabase now signs with ES256 asymmetric keys by default, so the original jsonwebtoken.verify(token, SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET) rejects every token; getClaims fetches/caches the JWKS and validates locally, keeping auth stateless.

nodes.label: user_addresses Table (BD shape, migration 038)
nodes.rationale: Reshaped from an original US-style schema in migration 038 to match the Bangladesh checkout shape exactly (label, name, phone, address, district, upazila) so saved addresses fill the order form directly.

nodes.label: order_items Table (price snapshot)
nodes.rationale: Snapshots product name/price at order time because products can be renamed or repriced; order history must reflect what the customer actually paid.

nodes.label: inventory Table (generated available_quantity)
nodes.rationale: available_quantity is a Postgres generated column (quantity - reserved_quantity), never stored, always consistent.

nodes.label: Key Indexes on High-Traffic Query Paths
nodes.norm_label: key indexes on high-traffic query paths

nodes.label: Row-Level Security Patterns
nodes.rationale: Public/owner/admin policies on original commerce tables; RLS-enabled-no-policies on backend-only tables (meta_capi_sent, kb_chunks, conversations, messages, courier_tracking_events); guest_sessions lacked RLS entirely until migration 040 closed the gap.

nodes.label: Build Order & Rationale
nodes.rationale: Modules built in strict dependency order so each module only depends on tables/APIs that already work, from Foundation through Categories/Brands/Products/Variants/Images/Cart/Orders/Inventory/Auth/AI Chat/Courier/Invoice PDF.

nodes.label: Transactional Stock Decrement (orders)
nodes.rationale: Order creation runs entirely in one DB transaction; stock decremented with an availability guard (quantity - reserved_quantity >= N) so concurrent checkouts cannot oversell; no separate reserve-then-decrement step.

nodes.label: AI Chat RAG Pipeline (rebuilt)
nodes.rationale: Rebuilt from a bare tool-use loop with simulated whole-block streaming into a real streaming RAG pipeline with pgvector retrieval, conversation persistence, sliding-window+intent-summary history, and retention cleanup.

nodes.label: CORS exposedHeaders (Content-Disposition)
nodes.rationale: Default CORS does not expose Content-Disposition to JS; without exposedHeaders the download filename falls back to a generic name.

nodes.label: fileParallelism: false Decision
nodes.rationale: Vitest 4 removed poolOptions.forks.singleFork; fileParallelism:false is the replacement, required because test files share one local Postgres instance and parallel execution causes FK violations.

nodes.label: Orders Use authLimiter Not strictLimiter
nodes.rationale: strictLimiter (5/min) would fail a test suite creating 6+ orders on the 6th request; authLimiter (20/15min) is permissive enough for tests while still production-appropriate.

nodes.label: Lazy Anthropic Client Initialization
nodes.rationale: Client initialized on first use, not module load, so vi.mock can intercept calls before the client is ever created without requiring ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in tests.

nodes.label: Real DB, No Mocks on Data Layer Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Mocks pass when production fails; the cart FK chain (cart_items -> profiles -> auth.users) is a real example a mocked test would never catch. Only the Anthropic SDK is mocked, to avoid burning API tokens in CI.
nodes.norm_label: real db, no mocks on data layer philosophy

nodes.label: FK-Safe Cleanup Order
nodes.rationale: Deletion between tests must respect FK constraints (courier events -> order_items -> orders -> cart_items -> variants -> products -> categories/brands); wrong order causes FK violations mid-test.

nodes.label: merge-back.yml Workflow
nodes.rationale: Squash-merges leave commits only on main, so dev drifts; this workflow checks out dev, merges origin/main, and pushes, failing the job on conflict for manual resolution.

nodes.label: Load Testing Plan Context (planned, not started)
nodes.rationale: No staging environment exists, so the intent is to load-test the full user journey against production directly; a codebase audit surfaced real risks (inventory/order pollution, real user rows, rate limiters, CORS not a safety boundary) the plan must work around.
nodes.norm_label: load testing plan context (planned, not started)

nodes.label: X-Load-Test-Run-Id Synthetic Tagging
nodes.rationale: A dedicated header flows through the request lifecycle and is stamped onto a nullable loadTestRunId column on orders -- exact tagging rather than heuristic SKU/email matching -- so cleanup and analytics filtering key off it directly, and gates side effects (email, Meta CAPI, Sentry) so synthetic traffic never leaks into real reporting.

nodes.label: Rate-Limiter Bypass Token
nodes.rationale: Opt-in LOAD_TEST_BYPASS_TOKEN follows the existing no-op-unless-configured convention (like Sentry/email); unset in production normally, set only for the scheduled test window.

nodes.label: Test Taxonomy (smoke/load/stress/soak)
nodes.rationale: Running a stress or soak test against shared production infra is materially riskier than a load test at expected-peak levels; plan runs smoke -> load only against prod, excluding stress/soak from the production target.

nodes.label: Ingestion Pipeline (knowledge.service.ts)
nodes.rationale: Deliberately not CDC -- fire-and-forget re-embed hook is simpler and sufficient at current catalog size. Policy-doc chunking was fixed to drop heading-only sections after a bug where title-only embeddings crowded out real content chunks.

nodes.label: Chat Guardrails (auth-gated get_my_orders)
nodes.rationale: get_my_orders only added to the tool list on an authenticated request, enforced in code and hard-scoped server-side to req.user.id; verified against an explicit prompt-injection attempt which was refused with no architectural path to succeed.

nodes.label: Product Card Matching (three-tier)
nodes.rationale: Cards must reflect what was said, not what was retrieved; matching can't depend on a tool call happening in the current turn (fallback via getAllProductTitles); matching can't be strict raw-string comparison due to messy catalog titles, so a whitespace-normalized exact tier plus a cleaned/fuzzy fallback tier with an anti-duplicate guard is used.

nodes.label: Retrieval Evaluation (pnpm eval:retrieval)
nodes.rationale: Measures precision/recall/hit-rate/MRR against a 32-query golden set across vector/hybrid/hybrid+rerank modes; hybrid+rerank strictly dominates (MRR 1.000) because the reranker demotes lexically-dense-but-irrelevant chunks hybrid's FTS leg promotes, and fixed the one ranking vector itself got wrong.

nodes.label: hybrid+rerank Retrieval Mode
nodes.rationale: pgvector cosine + Postgres FTS fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion, then reranked by Voyage rerank-2.5-lite (best-effort, 2500ms timeout, falls back to fusion order on failure); production default because it measured strictly >= vector across all metrics.

nodes.label: Answer-quality Evaluation (pnpm eval:answers)
nodes.rationale: LLM-as-judge scores the full streamChat answer against ground-truth reference facts on correctness/relevance/key-fact coverage/card accuracy/pass rate; unlike the saturated retrieval eval this one discriminates (pass rate 0.53), giving real headroom for prompt tuning.

nodes.label: Migration 039 CI paths-filter Incident
nodes.rationale: dorny/paths-filter's default push-event diff fell back to comparing live dev/main branch tips instead of the push event's fixed before/after SHAs, losing a race against merge-back.yml and silently skipping the migrate stage twice; fixed by pinning base/ref to github.event.before/github.sha.

links.label: EXTRACTED: pnpm-workspace.yaml declares both the package list and the allowBuilds approvals
